title: some namespaces and mount points won't cleaned up after the deletion of pod or container!?
body:
Hello,
I'm trying to setup the GlusterFS daemon in a container more specifically in a pod, the Containerfile is like below, 
```
FROM ubuntu:20.04

# Some enviroment variable to use by systemd 
ENV LANG C.UTF-8
ENV ARCH "x86_64"
ENV container docker
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive
# SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]

# Enable All Repositories For Further Usage
RUN sed -i 's/# deb/deb/g' /etc/apt/sources.list; 
RUN apt-get update -qq -y && \
    apt-get upgrade -qq -y && \
    apt-get install -qq -y --no-install-recommends \
    python python-apt \
    attr \
    net-tools dnsutils \
    nfs-kernel-server nfs-common \
    rsync openssh-server openssh-client \
    tar tzdata \
    iputils-ping iproute2 \
    software-properties-common apt-transport-https ca-certificates \
    wget rpcbind vim-tiny=2:8.1.*;

# Remove Some Unnecessary Staffs 
RUN apt-get purge -qq -y --auto-remove -o APT::AutoRemove::RecommendsImportant=false \
    && apt-get clean -qq -y;
RUN rm -rf /usr/share/doc/* /usr/share/man/* /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*;

# Add Add GlusterFS Repo GPG Key & The Repo Itself
RUN wget -O - https://download.gluster.org/pub/gluster/glusterfs/9/rsa.pub | apt-key add -;
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:gluster/glusterfs-9 -y;

# Install GlusterFS
RUN apt-get install -qq -y glusterfs-server glusterfs-client glusterfs-common;

# Config sshd
RUN mkdir /var/run/sshd;
RUN bash -c 'install -m755 <(printf "#!/bin/sh\nexit 0") /usr/sbin/policy-rc.d';
RUN ex +'%s/^#\zeListenAddress/\1/g' -scwq /etc/ssh/sshd_config;
RUN ex +'%s/^#\zeHostKey .*ssh_host_.*_key/\1/g' -scwq /etc/ssh/sshd_config;
RUN RUNLEVEL=1 dpkg-reconfigure openssh-server;
RUN ssh-keygen -A -v;
RUN update-rc.d ssh defaults;
RUN sed -i '/Port 22/c\Port 2222' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && \
    sed -i 's/PermitRootLogin prohibit-password/PermitRootLogin yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && \
    sed -i 's/#PasswordAuthentication yes/PasswordAuthentication no/g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && \
    sed 's@session\s*required\s*pam_loginuid.so@session optional pam_loginuid.so@g' -i /etc/pam.d/sshd;

EXPOSE 2222 111 245 443 24007 24008 2049 8080 6010 6011 6012 38465 38466 38468 38469 49152 49153 49154 49156 49157 49158 49159 49160 49161 49162

COPY --chown=root:root entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh;
# We customize how our app is loaded with the custom entrypoint:
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
CMD ["/entrypoint.sh"]
```
so when I build this Containerfile and run it with this command:
`podman run -d --name glusterfs --hostname glusterfs -v /data/volume1:/data/glusterfs/gvol-primary/brick1/ --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --cap-add NET_RAW --cap-add NET_ADMIN  -p 2222:2222,111:111,24007:24007,49152:49152 -t glusterfs9:local`
as I said in the first place I can mount a volume from my host itself but when I stop or delete the container and setup the container again and create an new volume, I can not mount anything again execpt when I delete the container and restart the server and try again, then it would work for the first time!!
in the container I create volume like this in a very simple way:
```
gluster volume create gvol-primary transport tcp glusterfs:data/glusterfs/gvol-primary/brick1/ force;
gluster volume start gvol-primary;
```
and in my host I run this command for the first time it will work and mount but if I umount and stop the container and then create or setup a new container and try to mount it again, I will get an error:
`mount -t glusterfs glusterfs:gvol-primary /storage;`
### **System information**
```
root@glusterfs:~# uname -a
Linux glusterfs 5.4.0-88-generic #99-Ubuntu SMP Thu Sep 23 17:29:00 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
root@glusterfs:~# podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.22.3
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpuset
  - cpu
  - cpuacct
  - blkio
  - memory
  - devices
  - freezer
  - net_cls
  - perf_event
  - net_prio
  - hugetlb
  - pids
  - rdma
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.30, commit: '
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "20.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: glusterfs
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.4.0-88-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 2196824064
  memTotal: 4127264768
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.1.5-7b4a-dirty
      commit: 7b4a7042370eea7fb00d3a4da34332b26f080acd
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.8
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.3
  swapFree: 8417959936
  swapTotal: 8417959936
  uptime: 13h 58m 15.04s (Approximately 0.54 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 2
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 28
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.3.1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 03:30:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.16.6
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.3.1
```
### **Expected Behavior**
if I mount again It should mount !!!!!!!!
I do this senario on docker platform it will work without any problem !!!!!!!!!!
### **Actual Behavior**
I can not mount and got errors with no reason.
```
root@glusterfs:~# mount -t glusterfs glusterfs:gvol-primary /storage
Mounting glusterfs on /storage failed.
```
some log file in the client side of glusterfs (in the host):
```
[2021-10-25 19:35:31.258104 +0000] I [MSGID: 101190] [event-epoll.c:669:event_dispatch_epoll_worker] 0-epoll: Started thread with index [{index=1}]
[2021-10-25 19:35:31.258134 +0000] I [MSGID: 101190] [event-epoll.c:669:event_dispatch_epoll_worker] 0-epoll: Started thread with index [{index=0}]
[2021-10-25 19:35:34.312090 +0000] I [glusterfsd-mgmt.c:2640:mgmt_rpc_notify] 0-glusterfsd-mgmt: disconnected from remote-host: glusterfs
[2021-10-25 19:35:34.312114 +0000] I [glusterfsd-mgmt.c:2662:mgmt_rpc_notify] 0-glusterfsd-mgmt: Exhausted all volfile servers
[2021-10-25 19:35:34.312331 +0000] W [glusterfsd.c:1428:cleanup_and_exit] (-->/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgfrpc.so.0(+0xffc5) [0x7f4323900fc5] -->/usr/sbin/glusterfs(+0x15642) [0x55f330262642] -->/usr/sbin/glusterfs(cleanup_and_exit+0x58) [0x55f3302567e8] ) 0-: received signum (1), shutting down
[2021-10-25 19:35:34.312364 +0000] I [fuse-bridge.c:7063:fini] 0-fuse: Unmounting '/storage'.
[2021-10-25 19:35:34.315310 +0000] I [fuse-bridge.c:7067:fini] 0-fuse: Closing fuse connection to '/storage'.
```
I believe that when I delete the container or stop it, some namespace like orphaned veth pairs reside in the host and somethings did not cleaned. I said this because when I delete the container the network namespace does not clean and I should do it by myself.

comments.body: Please provide the full information requested by the issue template - there's no way to proceed without the full output of `podman info`.
Also, you don't seem to have included the actual error that you're seeing upon attempting to mount the volume?
Hi Sir @mheon, I did an edit to my issue and provide more information as you mentioned.

Do you have any evidence that Podman is not cleaning up after the container? Does `podman container cleanup` on the container's ID return an error?

From what I see here, I tend to think that Gluster is modifying the host itself, given you are mounting over a volume mount from the host (and you've given the container full CAP_SYS_ADMIN).

Hmmm you know I didn't get anything from log files either from host or the daemon itself, glusterfs need that sort of cap but why this scenario did not happen in docker with the exact same cap and running configurations.
I run the command you specified and got no error but still this will happen.

I did a research and find out that glusterfs daemon use and set xattr on filesystem for creating a volume so I decide to run the container with diff source for brick and try to mount it again and I get error again so I believe that the default seccomp profile in podman and docker may cause this or the glusterfs client itself (which work in docker without a problem!)

Have you checked SELinux.  Any AVC messages. Docker may not be running with SELinux.
If it is SELinux or seccomp, then you would see messages in /var/log/audit/audit.log

For SELinux messages
# ausearch -m avc -ts recent
For Seccomp messages
# ausearch -m seccomp -ts recent
yes I haved but I'm in ubuntu so SELinux is disable by default. so I do not think that is the cause of this problem.
Did you check seccomp?

yes I even use the default **seccomp** profile of docker which is this file below:
`curl -LO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moby/moby/master/profiles/seccomp/default.json`
and then run the container with this command so that if this work fine on docker, maybe it would work in podman and the result was the same for the first try, it will mount. but for further attempt I will get that error again. the command is:
`podman run -d --name glusterfs --hostname glusterfs -v /data/volume1:/data/glusterfs/gvol-primary/brick1/brick --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --cap-add NET_RAW --cap-add NET_ADMIN -p 2222:2222,111:111,24007:24007,24008:24008,49152:49152 --security-opt seccomp=./default.json --security-opt apparmor=unconfined -t glusterfs9:local;`
the **apparmor** is disabled anyway...

Ok if it is not seccomp or selinux or apparmor. Then have you tried
Running podman as root?  If this works, then it is user namespace.
If not try running --privileged.
my containers are running as root, I'm not using rootless container for now. so yes podman is running as root. I'm forbid to use --privileged with my containers for security concerns.

Sure, but did it work as privileged?
If yes, could you try without --privileged but with `--cap-add=all`

I test it again with --privileged flag but still it doesn't work and also --cap-add=all flag wouldn't solve my problem.
I read all the logs of glusterfs client and server, but I got nothing useful ...
What does not work?
I mean, I can't mount the volume even with these flags enabled.

You are talking about mounting a gluster volume?  Or the gluster side.
I have a feeling this has something to do with the volume you are leaking into the container.

You might want to volume mount with the :shared or :slave options.

yes of course as you can see in my first comment I create a volume in the container where my glusterd is installed, and then start that volume. then I want to mount it outside of the container, in my host over the network. 
where should I put these option??? I didn't find anything related to these options!?
does you mean these options?
`https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/managing_file_systems/sharing-a-mount-on-multiple-mount-points_managing-file-systems`
`https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/filesystems/sharedsubtree.txt`
I run the container with this option on volume, like below:
`podman run -dit --name glusterfs --hostname glusterfs -v /data/volume1:/data/glusterfs/gvol-primary/brick1/brick:shared --cap-add=all -p 2222:2222 -p 111:111 -p 24007:24007 -p 49152:49152 -p 24008:24008 -t glusterfs9:local`
but again I can not mount the volume, I think that the conmon or CNI maybe the reason, does conmon behave exactly the way docker-proxy bind ports to the host? I said that because when I delete the pod or container the veth pairs of that container remain in the root namespace and wouldn't get cleaned.

@rhatdan @mheon, I have found the problem it is discussed in this issue too #5097 but how can I fix this problem? I mean when I delete the pod or container, all iptables rules should get clean immediately!?
What should I do so that all iptables rules corresponding to a deleted or stopped container got clean automatically?
Most likely the cleanup process is not firing. Please run Podman with `--log-level=debug` and provide any logs from Podman or Conmon in the system journal.

@rhatdan @mheon, Hi again I run some scenarios and here is my result, first I run the container like this:
`podman run -d --name glusterfs --hostname glusterfs -v /data/volume1:/data/glusterfs/gvol-primary/brick1/brick --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --cap-add NET_RAW --cap-add NET_ADMIN -v /dev/fuse:/dev/fuse -p 2222:2222,111:111,24007:24007,24008:24008,49152:49152 --log-level=debug -t glusterfs9:local`
the logs from stdout right away after I run is like this:

@rhatdan @mheon 
do you figure it out what is the problem with the cleanup process which is not firing after the deletion of pod/container? or how should I solved it for now except to cleanup them myself?

We need logs from the cleanup process, which are only printed to the system journal, not the terminal. Running with `--log-level=debug` turns on debugging for the cleanup process, which runs after Podman does and cleans up after the container.
Hi thanks but I uploaded the system journal logs in two .txt files, they are attached to the previous comment. or should I do something else?
It is not properly fixed but it should not cause such problems at the moment. The actual fix will be in 4.0.
so what should I do now? just wait for that version to be released...
@homayoonsadeghiarkami 4.0 should fix it entirely but there is also already a fix in dnsname, can you check the output of `/usr/libexec/cni/dnsname`. It should be at least version 1.3 IIRC.
Also `unset XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` should work when running as root. Note that you need to set this before you start the containers.
Closing since it is fixed on main. I assume your dnsname version is to old. How did you install podman?
@Luap99, thanks man I recompiled the dnsname package with the latest version and it worked seamlessly.
